outcomes: Apply Coulomb's law and superposition principle to calculate forces in charge systems.
Analyze the behavior of electric dipoles including torque in uniform electric fields.
Utilize Gauss's theorem to determine electric field intensity for symmetric charge distributions.
Differentiate between conductors, insulators, and dielectrics regarding polarization effects.
Calculate equivalent capacitance for series and parallel combinations of capacitors.
Derive expressions for energy stored in a capacitor using standard formulae.
Explain the relationship between drift velocity, mobility, and Ohm's law in conductors.
Analyze temperature dependence of resistivity and internal characteristics of cells.
Apply Kirchhoff's rules to solve complex circuits including Wheatstone bridge configurations.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Apply Biot-Savart law and Ampere's circuital law to calculate magnetic fields of current-carrying wires.
Determine the force exerted on a charge or conductor in an external magnetic field.
Understand the principle of converting a moving coil galvanometer into ammeters and voltmeters.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Classify magnetic materials based on qualitative properties such as susceptibility and permeability.
Model a bar magnet as an equivalent solenoid to explain its magnetic field lines.
Correlate the macroscopic magnetic moments of matter with atomic dipole alignments.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Explain Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction
Apply Lenz's Law to determine direction of induced current
Analyze self-inductance and mutual inductance phenomena

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Derive expressions for instantaneous and RMS values of AC
Analyze behavior of L, C, and R components in AC circuits
Calculate resonance frequency and Q-factor in series LC circuits
Describe the transverse nature of electromagnetic waves
Explain the relationship between electric and magnetic fields in EM waves
Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum based on frequency

learning_outcomes.topic: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments Competencies
outcomes: Apply Snell's Law to solve problems involving refraction at spherical surfaces
Derive the lens maker's formula for thin lenses
Explain the working principle of microscopes and telescopes
Explain Young's double-slit experiment and derive fringe width formula
Analyze conditions for constructive and destructive interference
Describe the phenomenon of polarization and its applications

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter Competencies
outcomes: Explain the experimental evidence for the particle nature of light through the photoelectric effect.
Describe de Broglie's hypothesis regarding the wave nature of matter particles.
Analyze the relationship between photon energy, frequency, and work function.
Evaluate Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and its implications for nuclear model.
Apply Bohr's postulates to explain the stability of atoms and hydrogen spectrum.
Identify different spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen) derived from electron transitions.
Calculate mass defect and binding energy per nucleon to explain nuclear stability.
Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes with real-world examples.
Analyze the characteristics of radioactive decay laws and half-life.

learning_outcomes.topic: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits Competencies
outcomes: Distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors based on doping types.
Explain the working principles of p-n junction diodes in rectification circuits.
Design simple logic gate combinations using basic transistor switches.
